---
title: "Experience"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
---

### Academic History

I received by undergraduate degree in psychology from the University of Colorado at Boulder in 2017 where I also received a certificate of public health. I'm often asked how I made the transition from psychology to biostatistics and data science. I was fortunate to be a part of a psychology program at CU that placed a lot of emphasis on research. This is how I first got my feet wet with statistics and R. From there, messing around with R became a hobby and ultimately pushed me down biostatistics path. 

During August of 2017, I moved from Boulder to New York City to start an MPH in biostatistics at Columbia University. Since arriving at Columbia, I have worked on research projects analyzing trends of marijuana use and the effects of medical marijuana and recreational marijuana policy on substance abuse. I'm also a teaching assistant for an introductory biostatistics and epidemiology course where I lead a weekly lab section of 25 first year MPH students. 

Relevant courses I have taken in both undergrad and graduate school: 

- blah   
- blah   
- more blah  

### Work History
